title: thymus hyemalis subsp. millefloris
intro: Thymus hyemalis subsp. millefloris, also known as winter thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its small, pinkish-purple flowers and distinctive fragrance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues. Thymus hyemalis subsp. millefloris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or essential oil, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
